query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
Research complete. Here's a summary of what I found on KOSPI foreign investor flows:

**Latest data (as of July 10, 2026):**
- Foreign investors have been **record net sellers** in 2026: **−148.32 trillion won in H1** and **−157.3 trillion won YTD through July 3** (~$102.5B).
- **Recent weekly trend (July 6–10)** turned choppy with tentative buying returns: after a 13-session selling streak (June 19–July 7), foreigners net-bought July 8 (+343.6B won) and July 9 (+134.8B won) — the first 2-day buy streak since June 12–16 — then sold again July 10 (−322.56B won).
- Daily flows in the latest week were in the **hundreds of billions of won**, much smaller than June's trillion-won swings (e.g., +2.2T won June 12).

**Key context:** Selling is concentrated ~90% in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, framed by KRX/brokerages as profit-taking after the KOSPI's 100%+ rally (peak 9,114.55 on June 22), amplified by won weakness. Retail investors absorbed the outflow (+104.8T won YTD). The full report with all figures, sources, and structured data points has been delivered.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Return, Fueling KOSPI Rebound Hopes
sources.snippet: Foreign investors returned to the market; July 8 +343.6B won, July 9 +134.8B won; first 2-day buy streak since June 12-16; 13 selling sessions June 19-July 7.

sources.snippet: Foreign investors sold 157.3 trillion won ($102.5B) YTD through July 3; foreign ownership rose to 40.47%; retail bought 104.8T won.

sources.title: Kospi Surges Over 100%, Yet Foreigners Dump Record $96.7 Billion
sources.snippet: Foreigners dumped record ~$96.7B of Kospi shares in H1 despite 100%+ index surge.

key_facts: Foreign investors net-sold a record 148.32 trillion won of KOSPI shares in H1 2026 (Jan 1-Jun 30), ~$95-106.8 billion.
YTD foreign net selling reached 157.3 trillion won ($102.5 billion) through July 3, 2026, per Korea Exchange.
Roughly ~9 trillion won of additional foreign net selling occurred in the first sessions of July (148.3T at June 30 vs 157.3T at July 3).
Foreigners net-sold for 13 consecutive sessions from June 19 to July 7, 2026.
Foreign flows turned positive July 8 (+343.6B won) and July 9 (+134.8B won) — first 2-day buy streak since June 12-16 — then sold July 10 (-322.56B won).
Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix accounted for ~90% of foreign H1 selling; Samsung foreign ownership hit a 16-year low.
KOSPI surged over 100% YTD, hitting an all-time high of 9,114.55 on June 22, 2026, before pulling back to ~7,300-7,475 by July 9-10.
Retail investors net-bought 104.8 trillion won YTD (through July 3), absorbing foreign selling in a 'hand-changing' pattern.
Foreign ownership of KOSPI rose to 40.47% on July 3, 2026, from 36.65% on Jan 2, 2026, despite net selling.
KRX CEO and brokerages attribute the selloff to profit-taking/rebalancing and won weakness, not loss of confidence.
In June, foreigners had strong buy days: +2.204T won (June 12), +1.0774T (June 15), +1.5353T (June 16); early June saw -1.24T (June 8).

data_points.metric: Foreign net selling KOSPI YTD through July 3
Retail net buying KOSPI YTD through July 3
